channel.title: Comments for page "How To Write An SCP"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "How To Write An SCP"

item.title: Unicode for Black Box
item.content:encoded:
Hello!

I'm unsure if this has already been suggested, as there are 29 pages of discussion on here, but the type of computer that I'm using doesn't take alt codes. It uses unicode instead (ctrl+shift+u). I don't know how many people have this sort of computer, but if you think it'd be helpful, the unicode is U+2588. (this would go under the formatting tab).

I'm not sure whether or not editing this would be allowed, and I don't feel confident enough with this syntax to do so. (I'm a newbie) Besides, I figure safe is better than sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
how do I do make an On-site link without using the actual designation of the SCP? I want it to be a key phrase that refers to the SCP's abilities, but it isn't working.

This is something to ask in the Questions Desk forum: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/c-891087/help:questions-desk

Provide a link to the sandbox in question/the code you're using so it's clearer to readers what you're trying to attempt here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Read and understood.
And also, how do I do make an On-site link without using the actual designation of the SCP? I want it to be a key phrase that refers to the SCP's abilities, but it isn't working.

item.title: Re: Posting section needs an update
item.content:encoded: Updated! Thanks for bringing that to staff attention.

item.title: Posting section needs an update
item.content:encoded: More specifically, the second footnote where it says "Series V" as a relevant Series, but currently it is Series VI

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Used to have a comment here, but I replaced it with what you're reading right now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would think that it would be harder to write thamiel class objects then keter class objects becuase it has to be usful to the foundation in containing other anomalies

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dr de Ruyter

item.title: Re: New greenlighting policy
item.content:encoded: Agreed; I have added a note about the new policy. Thank you!

item.title: Re: Looking for feedback and possible “publication”
item.content:encoded: If you can make it to #site17, we have chat operators who can explain the rest. IRC has been around for a long time and is extremely reliable.

item.title: New greenlighting policy
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't it be mentioned that your idea needs 2 greenlights in the last tab? This could cause confusion

item.title: Re: Looking for feedback and possible “publication”
item.content:encoded: I found out it, but the whole chatroom system is confusing as hell. Even with the step by step guide, I have no clue what I’m doing at all.

item.title: Re: Looking for feedback and possible “publication”
item.content:encoded: The left-hand sidebar has a "Chat with Us!" link: http://www.scp-wiki.net/chat-guide

item.title: Re: Looking for feedback and possible “publication”
item.content:encoded: How do I find/get to the ICR chatrooms?

item.title: Re: Looking for feedback and possible “publication”
item.content:encoded: Feedback should be sought in the IRC chatrooms or forums (as noted in this guide), not in the discussion thread for the guide itself.

item.title: Looking for feedback and possible “publication”
item.content:encoded:
http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/warlynx1

So I made my own SCP and just wanted some general feedback on it.

PS: If it is good enough, how do I submit it to the official SCP website?

item.title: Re: Help
item.content:encoded: You need to have a space after the greater-than-symbol on each line (including blank lines).

item.title: Help
item.content:encoded: The interview template isnt working when i try to repeat the interviewer and interviee it dose nothing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it reads fine as-is. Maybe imagine a comma after "illustration".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
“It is an illustration and realistically a photograph would be used instead”
Should be “It is an illustration where realistically a photograph would be used instead”

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
Moved some material from a current bullet point in the "Getting Ideas" section of the "Developing Thoughts" tab, to give the Ideas & Brainstorming its own tip.

Staff consensus from Silber, Dexanote, and TheDuckman.

item.title: Re: about drafts and the sandbox.
item.content:encoded:
so were would I write scp drafts

The sandbox.

I read the sandbox page an it states no scp 1234, 173 etc pages.

The sandbox directions state, "Do NOT create a sandbox title or URL with an SCP number. So no SCP-1234, no SCP-4321, no SCP-173 pages. Don't use anything starting with "SCP" and followed by several numbers."

It's telling you not to use "SCP-XXXX" as your page title. It is not telling you not to write SCP drafts.

item.title: Re: about drafts and the sandbox.
item.content:encoded: sorry sorry, I'm new here so can't tell my way around like I just said after editing the post

item.title: Re: about drafts and the sandbox..
item.content:encoded:
That's exactly what it is. And exactly what we say it is in the guide.

What is your question?

item.title: about writing drafts...
item.content:encoded: so were would I write scp drafts or is that closed until scps 4001-4999 are up? I'm new here so don't really know my way around but I read the sandbox page an it states no scp 1234, 173 etc pages.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Addressed! Thank you for notifying us of that fix!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
We have over 3000 SCPs

Update to "over 4000"? Found under the Developing Thoughts tab.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Addressed! Thank you for bringing that to staff attention. εїз

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Should update the series IV footnote to V

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: dub, i guess

item.title: Re: Time info?
item.content:encoded: Who are you replying to here? The context of your question is not clear.

item.title: Time info?
item.content:encoded: What kind of info do you mean. The day? Or the hour?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When are the SCP designations from 4001 to 4999 going to be open for use?

A little while after the SCP-4000 contest closes and Series IV is wrapped up. An announcement will be made then.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know sorry. When are the SCP designations from 4001 to 4999 going to be open for use?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How do I create tabs to separate my drafts? I've tried everything that I can and I can't figure it out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The community outreach team is slowly working on updating these links. As community members still use these links to get to the second sandbox site, we're holding off on changing everything in one fell swoop.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Shouldn't the links to the sandbox be for the third?

http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com

item.title: Re: D3 Foundation
item.content:encoded: Some say the Global Occult Coalition is sort of like that. http://www.scp-wiki.net/goc-hub-page

item.title: D3 Foundation
item.content:encoded: Am I the only one who thinks that sounds like something that actually exist, like an anti-SCP group, or simply an SCP division devoted to figuring out out how to destroy SCP like 682.

item.title: Re: Picture
item.content:encoded: Given that this discussion thread is over 27 pages and 400 comments long, a better place to ask would be the Questions Desk forum, where wiki syntax/coding queries are handled: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/c-891087/help:questions-desk

item.title: Picture
item.content:encoded: How do I add a picture to my SCP?

item.title: Re: Footnotes?
item.content:encoded: Given that this discussion thread is over 27 pages and 400 comments long, a better place to ask would be the Questions Desk forum, where wiki syntax/coding queries are handled: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/c-891087/help:questions-desk

item.title: Footnotes?
item.content:encoded: I'm confused on how to implement footnotes properly. Can someone give me a step-by-step on how to do that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
don't just say it does something

give the SCP a little bit of a story

Personally, I would say you are missing these.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: (Double post, apparently)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've attempted creating my own once, and oh boy did it fail. What am I missing?

Getting feedback on your drafts before posting. As mentioned in the 7th bullet point under the "Developing Thoughts" tab. Getting feedback on a SCP before posting is generally one of the most important steps- it gives you a chance to see where you're making mistakes and how to correct them.

Otherwise… well, you've already seen what generally happens if you post without getting feedback first.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Are you getting feedback on your idea, and on the draft, beforehand? Because that's a vital part of the step.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All that went through my head the first two times that I read this was this: Be specific, use grammar, make the dots connect, make readers understand why the special containment procedures is how it is, give the SCP a little bit of a story, don't just say it does something, don't make it short, and really that's all I can remember. I've attempted creating my own once, and oh boy did it fail. What am I missing?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All that went through my head the first two times that I read this was this: Be specific, use grammar, make the dots connect, make readers understand why the special containment procedures is how it is, give the SCP a little bit of a story, don't just say it does something, don't make it short, and really that's all I can remember. I've attempted creating my own once, and oh boy did it fail. What am I missing?

item.title: Re: Golden Rule?
item.content:encoded:
"Come up with a story first" tends to be more of a brainstorming technique that works for some people in some cases, rather than a you're-golden strategy to follow all the time.

I personally like having narratives in articles because that makes the immersion work better for me, but I also like shorter articles that aren't super heavy on the story aspect. Also, when I write I sometimes start with an object that I know I want to focus on, and develop a narrative based on interesting facts or legends about said object.

item.title: Re: Golden Rule?
item.content:encoded:
While I'm going to disagree with CWW that it's a meme from forum crit, I will agree that it's not a universal rule for writing an SCP.

I tell new users to think about the narrative they want their SCP to tell because it's something not a lot of new writers realize. Including a narrative won't suddenly make a new user a great writer, but it's a good first step.

item.title: Re: Golden Rule?
item.content:encoded: That's not, like, a universal way to go about writing an SCP. It's just a meme from the crit forums.

item.title: Golden Rule?
item.content:encoded:
During my brainstorm session in the IRC chat, I came across an interesting tip, which should be added as a Golden Rule.

Something like this:
Remember the SCP Writing Golden Rule:

Come up with a story you want to tell, and write an SCP around it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much, I've finally figured it out

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For every image file used, use the appropriate coding. If you need guidance, just look at the page sources for various articles that use images.

Page Source can be found by clicking 'Options' at the bottom of a Wikidot page, and then 'Page Source'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, so to format the picture you have to enter the text at the top of the article and any extra pictures just go anywhere? And how do you properly format it? I'm trying to understand but right now I have the brain capacity of a squid and i can't figure it out at all

item.title: needs to be updated
item.content:encoded: This page still says that series III is the current SCP series, which is as of now outdated. Unfortunately, I can't edit the page, if so I would have done it myself. Just a heads up. :)

item.title: Re: Cross linking help!
item.content:encoded: This sort of question should be asked in a thread in the Questions Desk forum: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/c-891087/help:questions-desk

item.title: Cross linking help!
item.content:encoded: Hello there, I have made a nice little SCP and upon reading noticed that 1 SCP had a very tiny minor similarity. With this I would like to briefly mention the SCP but do not have a clear understanding of Cross Linking.
